verdict_reasoning: SRC is canonically inner-leaflet-anchored via N-myristoylation and polybasic-cluster electrostatic binding to acidic phospholipids; it is not surface-accessible at baseline. However, documented in vivo and in vitro evidence shows that SRC undergoes noncanonical translocation to the outer leaflet of cancer cell plasma membranes via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (ALE), becoming functionally inverted and accessible to surface-directed modalities in malignant cells. This cancer-cell-restricted ecto-presentation qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface accessibility.
